title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: - **Critical correctness risk**: Quorum-stop success can bypass required Claude+GLM gate. In `launch`, provider verification runs only when `child_rc == 0`; if canary stops child via SIGTERM (`143/-15`), code still returns success when `quorum_satisfied && audit_exit==0`. This can produce a false “multi-model pass.” [cli.py#L554](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L554) [cli.py#L583](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L583) - **Critical policy gap**: “required phase evidence” is optional by default (`()`), so quorum-stop can pass without phase proof unless explicitly configured. [config.py#L28](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/config.py#L28) [session_watch.py#L347](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L347) - **High false-positive risk**: phase evidence is inferred from `spawn_agent` message text patterns, not from actual lane outcomes. Early stop increases the chance of “declared phases” passing without substantive execution. [session_watch.py#L115](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L115) [session_watch.py#L124](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L124) [session_watch.py#L229](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/session_watch.py#L229) - **High reliability risk**: current “success on quorum” path already exists (`CODEX_KERNEL_SUCCESS_ON_QUORUM`) and depends on monitor message string matching + signal handling; this is brittle if wording or exit-code behavior changes. [cli.py#L475](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L475) [cli.py#L594](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L594) - **Medium flake risk**: intentional termination assumes child exits as `143/-15` (or `0`), otherwise canary may fail nondeterministically despite valid quorum. [cli.py#L583](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py#L583) - **Test gap**: there is no visible coverage for quorum-stop + provider-gate interaction (especially the `143` path), so regressions here will likely escape. [tests/test_watch.py#L748](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/tests/test_watch.py#L748)
